headline: Penn State edges Georgia in turnover battle, Myers steady in upset bid
body: Penn State's defense forced the issue when it mattered most, converting Georgia's two fumbles into the margin of victory in a 29-26 win that hinged on possession discipline rather than yardage. Georgia actually out-gained Penn State 335 to 327 and held a slight edge in passing yards through 41 completions, yet the Bulldogs' two giveaways proved fatal in a game decided by three points.

Mallik Myers orchestrated the winning effort with surgical precision despite completing just 22 of 36 attempts. His two touchdown passes and lone interception-free performance gave Penn State's coaching room exactly what they needed: a quarterback who protected the football when turnovers were the currency of the day. Georgia's Giles matched Myers' touchdown total and threw for 254 yards, but the two fumbles—one recovered by each team—shifted momentum at critical junctures.

The passing game dominated the script. Georgia's Nash hauled in six catches for 94 yards, stretching the Penn State secondary repeatedly, while Penn State's Hinnant worked underneath routes to move the chains. Neither team found consistent success on the ground, with Penn State managing just 98 rushing yards and Georgia 90, forcing both offenses into third-down situations where execution separated them. Penn State converted when it counted; Georgia did not.
turningPoint: Georgia's second fumble in the fourth quarter, recovered by Penn State deep in Georgia territory, eliminated the Bulldogs' comeback window and forced them into desperation mode with limited time remaining.

unsungHero.name: Kelsay
unsungHero.why: Six tackles and rock-solid coverage accountability in the secondary, without the sack or splashy play that garners headlines, but his consistency limited Georgia's explosive passing opportunities and kept drives manageable.
